title: athyrium filix-femina f. laciniatum
intro: Athyrium filix-femina f. laciniatum, also known as the lady fern, is a subspecies of the common lady fern plant, which is native to Europe, Asia, and North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The lady fern is believed to have several health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving respiratory issues, as well as acting as an antioxidant and a diuretic. Herbal preparations of the lady fern include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to treat a range of ailments, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness.
